englishDefinition: to publicly recommend or support a particular cause or policy
exampleSentence: Many doctors now advocate a Mediterranean diet for patients with heart disease.
exampleSentenceHebrew: רופאים רבים תומכים כיום בדיאטה ים-תיכונית למטופלים עם מחלות לב.
commonTrap: Easy to confuse with the noun form 'advocate' (a person who advocates). As a verb, 'advocate' takes a direct object without 'for' in formal writing: 'advocate a policy', not 'advocate for a policy'.
